promotion_tool: governed dev→release promotion (copy allow-listed changed files + ZIP)
store_upload: NONE — no Chrome Web Store upload performed by this promotion
github_release: NONE — no GitHub Release created by this promotion
wildcard_host_permissions: NONE — AG-363 removed https://*/* from manifest.chrome.json and manifest.firefox.json (unchanged by this promotion)
note: Governed dev→release promotion of AG-PROMPT-369 (Copilot drag/drop Cancel bypass fix), AG-PROMPT-370 (Firefox DOCX extraction parity fix), and AG-PROMPT-371 (Firefox XLSX/PPTX extraction parity fix + centralized realm-copy helper). Only two allow-listed files changed since the AG-365/AG-366 promotion lineage: src/content/index.ts and src/content/metadataExtractor.ts (confirmed via git diff against the AG-365 source commit 0c6566f, restricted to the full AG-269 allow-list — no other allow-listed file touched). AG-369: handleDrop's event.stopPropagation() replaced with event.stopImmediatePropagation() — a same-node/same-phase competing host listener (the confirmed root cause of Copilot's drag/drop Cancel not preventing host receipt) is now correctly suppressed; Cancel proves host non-receipt/no-attachment (not merely modal-hidden), Continue still delivers exactly one file via the pre-existing shared file-input injection path. No MAIN-world code added or required. Copilot paste and file-picker were already unaffected and remain covered. AG-370/371: extractDocxMetadataSelective, extractXlsxMetadataSelective, and extractPptxMetadataSelective now route bytes through one shared toRealmSafeBytes() same-realm deep-copy helper before any JSZip.loadAsync() call (mirroring the pre-existing PDF extraction defense against the identical Firefox Xray-wrapper class of bug). Without this copy, a foreign-realm ArrayBuffer/Uint8Array view crossing the Firefox content-script/page boundary can fail JSZip's internal instanceof Uint8Array/ArrayBuffer checks, silently degrading extraction to success:false (no signals, no warning) on Firefox only — Chrome was never affected. Legacy .doc/.xls/.ppt remain explicitly unsupported (no dispatch branch exists for any of the three; unchanged by this promotion, not newly claimed as supported). No manifest, permission, or host-scope change (AG-363 narrowing carried forward unchanged — permissions: ['storage'] only; host_permissions limited to the 6 listed patterns; no activeTab; no wildcard/https://*/*; no <all_urls>; connect-src 'none'). No RiskSignal/deduplication/chunking structure change. No detection threshold change. No telemetry, network diagnostics, or centralized logging added. No raw document content, file names, or personal data logged or persisted (production bundle strips all console.log/console.debug via terser pure_funcs, verified). Dev-side verification (this promotion): tsc 0 errors; CORE 51/51 (incl. test-copilot-drop-coverage 17/17, test-firefox-docx-parity 13/13, test-office-zip-realm-copy 23/23); e2e 49/49 (drag-drop incl. both AG-369 Copilot-pattern window-capture Cancel/Continue tests, file-picker, paste, docx-upload from AG-370); Chrome build 34/34 + 20/20 consumer-copy; Firefox build 25/25. Firefox live-runtime e2e (interception-smoke.firefox.spec.ts) remains environment-blocked — 3 skipped, re-verified unchanged in this run (pre-existing AG-213 geckodriver 0.37.0 / Firefox 151.0.2 extension-injection incompatibility, not a new failure and not caused by these fixes). The Firefox DOCX/XLSX/PPTX and Copilot fixes are therefore structurally proven (source-level correctness via Node/tsx extraction+detection tests, structural realm-copy presence, and Chrome-equivalent headed-browser e2e proving the shared code path end-to-end) but not yet operator-confirmed on a real Firefox browser runtime — recommended before/alongside upload. Release gate: 10/10 (type-check, build:chrome incl. artifact A-D, package:chrome incl. no-maps/no-TS guards, license UX, no-activity-record, message-handler safety, storage manager, storage hardening, signal-manifest integrity, manifest verification). Final ZIP artifact hygiene: clean — 14 entries (8 real files: manifest.json, content.js, background.js, popup.html, warning-modal.html, warning-modal.css, _locales/en/messages.json, 4 icon PNGs), no .map/.ts/.tsx files, no docs/reports or governance files bundled, no AG-PROMPT-ID leakage in shipped content.js/background.js/popup.html/manifest.json (grep-confirmed absent), no full dev URL literals (http://127.0.0.1, http://localhost — grep-confirmed absent; a pre-existing, unrelated bare-hostname string in content.js is a detection-layer false-positive-suppression heuristic in src/detection/qualityGates.ts, not a dev artifact), console.log/console.debug fully stripped (D.1-D.4), no unguarded Node-only globals (AG-356 guard active), event.stopImmediatePropagation() present exactly twice in shipped content.js (matching the 2 real code call sites in source — AG-369's handleDrop fix plus the pre-existing untrusted-synthetic-click guard; the 3 additional source occurrences are inside comments and correctly do not survive minification). Permissions: ['storage'] only; no wildcard host access; connect-src 'none'. No store upload; no GitHub Release. No website repo modified.
